sentence_reviews.text: Visibility constraints define which positions or features can interact or must be predicted.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning supports broad regularization, sequence, and representation-learning background; masking-specific support is indirect.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition is consistent with causal and masked modeling, but direct verification needs masking-specific sources.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Causal, masked & structured masking is to connect Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units, Sequences, events & time series to downstream capabilities including Encoder–decoder transformers, Decoder-only causal transformers, Joint-embedding predictive architectures.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support token/sequence background but not the full downstream transformer and JEPA list.
sentence_reviews.notes: Causal and masked modeling are relevant, but the downstream list needs specific citations.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support sequence modeling background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Attention masks are common in encoder-decoder transformers, but the packet source is not direct.

sentence_reviews.text: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units, Sequences, events & time series.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support token and sequence background, not the generic limitation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dependencies are plausible; the limitation statement is not directly sourced.

sentence_reviews.text: Causal, masked & structured masking uses Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supports symbolic and sequence modeling background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Token masking is a central case, though structured masks may also apply beyond tokens.

sentence_reviews.text: Causal, masked & structured masking uses Sequences, events & time series as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supports probabilistic sequence and time-series modeling background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Causal masks are sequence-order constraints, making this dependency defensible.

sentence_reviews.text: Causal, masked & structured masking uses Images, video & spatial grids as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited source provides broad data/modal background but not direct spatial masking evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: Spatial masks are common in vision and video, but this common dependency needs a more specific masked-vision source.

edge_reviews.notes: Token masking is a core case of this mechanism.
Causal masking depends on ordered sequence structure; core strength is defensible.
Structured spatial masks are common, and common strength is defensible though direct source support is absent.
